CABINET: a cupboard with doors and shelves
CACHE: a collection of items stored in hidden place
CAJOLE: to persuade by coaxing or flattery
CALL: to shout or cry out; to telephone
CALM: peaceful and quiet; not showing excitement
CAMARADERIE: mutual trust and friendship among people
CAMOUFLAGE: to hide by blending with surroundings
CANDOR: the quality of being open and honest
CANOPY: a cloth covering suspended above bed or throne
CAPITAL: the most important city; wealth used in business
CAPRICIOUS: given to sudden changes of mood or behavior
CAPTIVATE: to attract and hold interest of
CAPTIVE: a person who has been taken prisoner
CAPTURE: to take into one's possession or control
CARBON: the chemical element that forms diamonds
CAREER: an occupation undertaken for significant period
CARGO: goods carried on ship or aircraft
CARVE: to cut hard material to produce object
CATCH: to capture; to grasp moving object
CATEGORIZE: to place in particular class or group
CAUSTIC: able to burn through chemical action; sarcastic
CAVITY: an empty space within solid object
CEASE: to bring or come to an end
CELL: the smallest structural unit of organism
CENSOR: to examine and suppress unacceptable parts
CERTAINTY: firm conviction that something is case
CHAIN: a connected series of metal links
CHALLENGE: a call to take part in contest
CHAMPION: a person who fights for cause
CHARACTERISTIC: a feature typical of person or thing
CHARGE: to ask for payment; to rush forward
CHARITABLE: generous in giving help to those in need
CHARITY: help given to those in need
CHARM: the power of delighting or attracting
CHART: a graph showing information; to map
CHASE: to pursue in order to catch
CHOICE: an act of selecting between alternatives
CHRONICLE: a factual written account of events
CITATION: a quotation from or reference to book
CLAIM: to state that something is case
CLASSIFY: to arrange in classes or categories
CLEAVE: to split or sever along grain
CLOSE: near in space or time; to shut
COAST: the part of land near sea
COAT: an outer garment; a layer covering
COERCE: to persuade using force or threats
COEXIST: to exist at same time or place
COGNITION: the mental process of acquiring knowledge
COHESION: the action of forming united whole
COLLAPSE: to fall down or cave in
COLLEAGUE: a person with whom one works
COLLECTIVE: done by people acting as group
COMMUNITY: a group living in same place
COMPANION: a person one spends time with
COMPARABLE: able to be likened to another
COMPARATIVE: measured in relation to something else
COMPATIBLE: able to exist together without conflict
COMPEL: to force someone to do something
COMPENSATE: to give something to make up for loss
COMPLIANCE: the action of conforming to rules
COMPLY: to act in accordance with rules
COMPOSE: to write or create; to constitute
CONCERN: a matter of interest or importance
CONCLUDE: to bring to an end; decide
CONCLUSION: the end or finish of event
CONCUR: to be of same opinion; agree
CONCURRENT: existing or happening at same time
CONDITION: the state that someone is in
CONFORM: to comply with rules or standards
CONJECTURE: an opinion formed on incomplete information
CONSCIOUSNESS: the state of being awake and aware
CONSEQUENCE: a result of action or condition
CONSIDERABLE: notably large in size or amount
CONSTRAIN: to compel by physical or moral force
CONTEMPT: the feeling that someone is worthless
CONTENT: pleased with situation; substance of something
CONTRACT: a written agreement; to decrease in size
CONTRIBUTE: to give to help achieve goal
CONVOLUTED: extremely complex and difficult to follow
COOPERATE: to work jointly toward same end
COORDINATE: to bring different elements into relationship
CORRUPT: having or showing willingness to act dishonestly
COUNTER: to speak or act in opposition to
CRAFTY: clever at achieving goals by deceit
CREATION: the action of bringing something into existence
CREATURE: an animal as distinct from human
CREDENCE: belief in or acceptance of something
CREDULOUS: having too great readiness to believe
CRITERIA: principles by which something is judged
CRITICIZE: to indicate faults in disapproving way
CULTIVATE: to prepare land for growing crops
CUNNING: skilled in achieving goals by deceit
CURRENT: belonging to present time; flow of water
CURVE: line that deviates from being straight
CUT: to make opening with sharp edge
